results.title: Scribble Network: The product that makes AI recommend your brand
results.content:
So we built the thing nobody else has: the first AI Search tool married to a creator platform.

We don't just measure your AI visibility, we fix it.

Our product attributes each brand mention back to the exact creator post that earned it.

And the platform now pays creators when they get cited. Creators love this.

This is our edge. We came at this from distribution and not measurement.   
  
We never asked 'how do we track this?', we asked 'how do we actually get brands cited?'  
  
The answer to this already lives in Scribble; try it on your own brand and tell me what you find.

Honest feedback wanted.

My co-founders and I are here all day answering everything.

Mohsin Ali
PicWish

PicWish

Hello Fam, [...] kaavya prasad

@hazy0 We're going the smart contract route for this. We will write scribbler <> citation data on chain with the copy of the answer and the engine, with a timestamp on a public blockchain. This will trigger a USDC payment instantly as the conditions are met. All logs will be viewable on a block explorer - we'll obviously make it a bit more readable. And yes - this is useful for creators as it stands now. But over time this data graph builds, and we could potentially have an industry <> Creator citation probability that is verifiable. Brands might really like that. Thinking along these lines at the moment.

Hazy

results.title: Scribble - LinkedIn
results.content: go check out the blog on Scribble Network. [Likes: 3, Comments: 0]; Scribble (1w): What does it take for a brand to start showing up in AI search? For RocketX, the answer was 15 days, 162 creators, and 358 pieces of distributed creator content. Before the campaign, RocketX had a strong product differentiator: a cross-chain aggregator combining CEX and DEX liquidity in one interface. But AI platforms were not surfacing that positioning in high-intent category prompts. In other words, the product was there. The AI citation footprint was not. Through Scribble Network, creators published independent content across X, Substack, Medium, Reddit, and Paragraph, focusing on comparison pieces, privacy-first swap narratives, and user trust stories. The result: • 9% AI Share of Voice from near-zero • [...] Scribble Network is a global community of creators, researchers, and storytellers working together to make complex ideas more accessible through high-quality content. Operating at the intersection of advertising and emerging technology, we collaborate with tech, fintech, and internet-native brands to turn products, protocols, and digital trends into clear, engaging narratives. Instead of traditional influencer marketing, Scribble Network runs structured creator campaigns that reward clarity, originality, and depth — not just audience size. For creators, Scribble Network offers transparent earning opportunities, thoughtful briefs, and a peer community focused on craft, learning, and shared growth. For brands, it’s a way to work with informed creators who can explain, contextualize, and [...] important. Read the full guide below.  [Likes: 1, Comments: 1]; Scribble (1w): A strong product is not enough if AI search cannot find it. That was the problem RocketX came in with. RocketX had a clear differentiator: a cross-chain aggregator combining CEX and DEX liquidity in one interface. But across high-intent prompts on ChatGPT, Grok, Gemini, Perplexity, and Copilot, that positioning was barely showing up. So we ran a 15-day GEO sprint. Through Scribble Network, 162 independent creators published 358 pieces of content across X, Substack, Medium, Reddit, and Paragraph. The goal was simple: Make RocketX’s hybrid CEX + DEX model impossible to miss inside AI search. The results: • 9% AI Share of Voice from near-zero • 123 tracked queries covered • 38 citations and 113 mentions •

results.title: Scribble | Monitoring and building AI visibility
results.content:
AI

ChatGPT doesn't recommend your brand. Scribble fixes that.

UGC Campaigns For  
AI Search Visibility.

Creator content is a cheat code for citation and mentions.

Your customers are asking ChatGPT, Perplexity, or Gemini who to trust in your category. The brands they recommend didn't get there with ads. If real people aren't writing about your brand, the answer won't be you. If real people aren't writing about your brand, the answer won't be you.

Book a callGet Free GEO Report

### Track And Compare

See how you stack up against your competitors. Track citations and mentions across every major AI model in real time.

Track And Compare

### Content Gaps

Spot the queries where competitors show up in AI and you don't. Turn every gap into a targeted creator campaign.

Content Gaps [...] What is a citation score and how do I improve mine?

Your citation score measures how often AI assistants reference your brand when users ask questions in your category. You improve it by increasing the volume, quality, and reach of indexed content about your brand, which is exactly what a Scribble campaign does, month on month.

## In the news

Scribble Blog

Scribble AI: How Brands Can Make Themselves AI-Discoverable In The New Search Era

### Scribble AI: How Brands Can Make Themselves AI-Discoverable In The New Search EraReclaim Protocol Blog

How Scribble Network Gets Creator Data Without APIs or Scraping

### How Scribble Network Gets Creator Data Without APIs or ScrapingAlexa Blockchain Blog

Scribble: Rethinking Distribution and AI-Driven Discovery in Web3 [...] How does the AI discoverability piece actually work?

When creators publish content about your brand, Scribble structures it into indexed knowledge clusters, microsites, hub pages, structured FAQs, that search engines crawl and AI models pull from when answering relevant queries. It's not about one viral post. It's about building a web of consistent, citable, AI-readable content around your brand.

What platforms does Scribble publish content on?

X (Twitter), Reddit, YouTube, Instagram, Medium, Quora, and other AI-citable platforms. These are the sources AI models actively pull from when forming answers, which is exactly why we focus on them.

What is a citation score and how do I improve mine?

results.title: Today, we're launching the Scribble dashboard. For the past few ...
results.content:
It brings together AI search intelligence, creator campaign tracking, citation monitoring, and visibility reporting in one place.  
  
So brands can stop guessing where they stand and start seeing exactly how their presence is moving across ChatGPT, Perplexity, Grok, Gemini, Copilot, and beyond.  
  
This is the same workflow we’ve been using internally to help brands get cited, mentioned, and trusted across the internet.  
  
Now we’re opening it up.  
  
The goal is simple:  
  
Help brands understand what they should be known for, create content worth citing, activate real creators to carry it, and track whether it is actually working.  
  
Scribble is live on Product Hunt. Going onchain with Sui Network.   
  
Come take a look. Link in the bio.   
  
#producthunt #scribbleai [...] Today, we’re launching the Scribble dashboard.  
  
For the past few months, we’ve been running creator-led AI visibility campaigns and tracking how brands show up across AI search.  
  
One thing became clear very quickly: AI visibility is no longer something brands can manage with screenshots, manual checks, and scattered campaign reports.  
  
Teams need to know:  
  
• What their brand is being cited for?  
  
• Which queries they are missing from?  
  
• Which creators are driving visibility?  
  
• Which AI engines are picking them up?  
  
• How they compare against competitors?  
  
• And what to do next?  
  
That is what the Scribble dashboard is built to answer. [...] Photo by Scribble on June 24, 2026. May be an image of text that says 'CASESTUDY CASE STUDY SCRIBBLE HELPS BRANDS WITH THEIR AI VISIBILITY This study sampled 1,504 citations produced across 25 queries for cross-chain DEX aggregators on ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Grok. All percentages and domain counts reflect this sample. scribble.network scribble. .network 2026'.
